name: Jigarthanda
description: Jigarthanda is a famous South Indian cooling drink that originated in Madurai, Tamil Nadu. The name literally means "cool heart" (Jigar = heart, Thanda = cool) and it is renowned for its refreshing taste and body-cooling benefits. Traditionally made with reduced milk (khoya), badam pisin (almond gum), nannari syrup and a special khoya ice cream, this rich, layered beverage is a summer favourite. The iconic pinkish ice cream and jelly-like texture make it a unique treat.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: divided for khoya, caramel and ice cream

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: for blooming badam pisin and serving

steps.instruction: Soak badam pisin in 1 cup water overnight or for at least 8 hours. It will bloom into a jelly-like mass. Refrigerate until use.
steps.tip: Soak badam pisin for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best results.

steps.instruction: Take 2 litres milk in a large pot. Add ½ cup sugar and bring to a full boil.
Cook for 20 minutes until reduced by half. Leave about 1½ cups milk in the pot and remove the rest into a bowl. Set aside.
Continue boiling the milk in the pot until it thickens into a khoya-like texture. Add 2 more tbsp sugar to help khoya form faster.
In a separate pan, heat 3 tbsp sugar. Swirl the pan (do not stir) until it melts and turns dark caramel colour.
steps.tip: Swirl the pan for caramel — never stir or it will crystallize.

steps.instruction: Pour the reserved milk into the khoya. Add the caramel and mix well.
Remove ⅓ of this mixture into a separate bowl. This is your reduced milk for serving. Refrigerate until needed.
To the remaining reduced milk, add ½ cup cream and 1 tsp vanilla. Mix well.
Pour into a freezer-safe container and freeze for 6 hours.
steps.tip: The ice cream can be made a week in advance and stored in the freezer.

steps.instruction: Once frozen, scoop into a blender and blend for 2 to 3 minutes until creamy.
Pour back into the container and freeze again until solid. Repeat once more for extra creamy ice cream.
In a tall glass, add 2 to 3 tbsp soaked badam pisin.
Top with ½ cup regular chilled milk.
Add a scoop of khoya ice cream.
steps.tip: Some restaurants use vanilla ice cream for convenience, but khoya ice cream gives the authentic taste.

steps.instruction: Drizzle more nannari syrup on top. Add crushed ice if desired.

steps.tip: Consume immediately after mixing — do not let it sit.

tips: The ice cream can be made a week in advance and stored in the freezer.
Soak badam pisin for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best results.
You can use store-bought nannari syrup to save time.
Swirl the pan for caramel — never stir or it will crystallize.
The pinkish colour of the ice cream comes from the caramel added to the milk.
Some restaurants use vanilla ice cream for convenience, but khoya ice cream gives the authentic taste.
Consume immediately after mixing — do not let it sit.
